source.title: Accident Investigation Report - South Pass 27 #5 Platform Allision

context.region: Gulf of America, South Pass Block 27

event.costs: {"amount": 25000, "currency": "USD", "description": "Less than $25,000. The platform is not in consideration for repair due to future decommissioning plans."}
actions_taken: MV crew assessed damage to vessel and platform
Island Operators personnel dropped off at WD 106 platform
MV proceeded to Allied Shipyard in Golden Meadow, Louisiana for repairs
Drug testing performed on all MV crew (all negative)
Witness statements collected by GOL representatives
Safety alert to be shared with GOL fleet regarding maneuvering close to platforms/structures
Review of navigation policy to be held with entire GOL fleet
event.summary: On 18 July 2025 at 0730 hours, the Motor Vessel Marc C allided with South Pass 27 #5 platform during platform surveying operations for decommissioning. The vessel captain set course to West Delta field approximately 3 minutes after leaving East Bay field without properly clearing all structures. The captain was distracted by computer work and failed to use the electronic navigation chart system, violating company safe navigation policy. The allision caused damage to both the platform structure and the starboard bow of the vessel. No injuries or pollution occurred.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations should consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
GOL to share safety alert with fleet for maneuvering close to platforms/structures and safe navigation
GOL to hold review of navigation policy with each vessel of entire fleet
key_phrases: electronic navigation chart system not used
violation of company safe navigation policy
failure to account for proximity of navigational hazards

hazards.name: Moving Vessel in Proximity to Fixed Offshore Structures

threats.name: Vessel navigation without proper situational awareness
Failure to use electronic navigation systems

controls.name: Electronic Navigation Chart System
controls.barrier_role: Prevent vessel collision through real-time structure identification
controls.lod_basis: Primary technical system to identify navigational hazards

supporting_text: The captain did not use his electronic navigation chart system that has the structures in the field clearly identified
This is a violation of the company safe navigation/voyage planning policy

controls.name: Company Safe Navigation/Voyage Planning Policy
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper navigation procedures are followed
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement for safe vessel operation

supporting_text: The captain violated company safe navigations/voyage planning policy by setting his course to the WD field prior to clearing all structures in the East Bay field
Not following proper procedures- The MV captain did not use his electronic navigation chart system

controls.name: Visual Lookout by Captain
controls.barrier_role: Maintain continuous visual awareness of navigational hazards
controls.lod_basis: Primary human monitoring during vessel operation

supporting_text: The captain stated that after leaving the East Bay field, he looked out the wheelhouse and did not see any structures in his view. He then set his course to the WD field and was traveling at approximately 8 knots. The captain then turned around to do paperwork on his computer
Inattention to task- The boat captain was not paying attention to the MV's heading once he set course
focusing his attention on his computer rather than navigating the vessel while underway

controls.name: Designated Lookout Personnel
controls.barrier_role: Provide additional visual monitoring when captain is occupied

supporting_text: the captain allowing himself to become distracted by working on the computer and not designating proper look-out personnel while he did his computer work
One witness stated that they were in the wheelhouse with the captain at the time of the incident but had their back turned away from the bow of the MV

controls.name: Pre-voyage Hazard Assessment
controls.barrier_role: Identify and account for navigational hazards before setting course
controls.lod_basis: Planning activity to prevent exposure to hazards

supporting_text: Not aware of hazards- The captain of the MV failed to identify the hazards of nearby structures before setting the MV's course to his next location
the failure to account for the proximity of navigational hazards

controls.name: Post-incident Drug Testing
controls.barrier_role: Verify crew fitness and rule out substance impairment as causal factor

supporting_text: Upon arrival at the dock the MV crew was drug tested and provided statements to Gulf Offshore Logistics, L.L.C. (GOL) representatives. The results of all drug tests performed came back negative

controls.name: USCG Notification and Response Protocol
controls.barrier_role: Ensure regulatory notification and safe vessel movement post-incident

supporting_text: The MV immediately notified the United States Coast Guard (USCG) of the incident that had occurred
The USCG then gave the MV permission to proceed to drop off the Island Operators at WD 106 platform and travel to the shipyard for repairs

controls.name: Damage Assessment Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Evaluate extent of damage and determine safe actions post-incident

supporting_text: After the MV contacted the platform, the MV crew began assessing the damage on the MV as well as the SP 27 #5 platform
During the assessment, the crew confirmed damage to the platform, as well as the starboard bow of MV. There were no injuries to personnel on the MV

controls.name: Internal Investigation and Root Cause Analysis
controls.barrier_role: Identify causal factors and prevent recurrence
controls.lod_basis: Organizational learning and corrective action development

supporting_text: The GOL internal investigation concluded that multiple factors could have contributed to the incident
GOL has stated that they will take multiple steps to prevent this type of incident from occurring in the future

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
